title: static ip address is already allocated, but that container is already deleted
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**
I am replacing a container with a new one with the same statically assigned IP.

Starting the new container gives:
```
$ podman start 9aa                                                                    
ERRO[0000] Starting some container dependencies         
ERRO[0000] "IPAM error: requested ip address 2a07::redacted is already allocated to container ID c4bf4b75204ab3abba9e950baf52aa2de6aa169dea1d7ca000dc4ce4c57b76ef" 
Error: unable to start container "9aa4f3c7acc19e3314af787d246a03b8730b5d5d32b277747fa219bafd0e7a7e": error starting some containers: internal libpod error
```
however the old container no longer exists:
```
$ podman container rm c4bf4b75204ab3abba9e950baf52aa2de6aa169dea1d7ca000dc4ce4c57b76ef
Error: no container with name or ID "c4bf4b75204ab3abba9e950baf52aa2de6aa169dea1d7ca000dc4ce4c57b76ef" found: no such container
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Not sure, think the container restarted a lot and there's a race condition freeing the IP somewhere?

A reboot fixed it.

**Describe the results you received:**
IP address allocated of nonexistent container

**Describe the results you expected:**
new container starts without issue

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 14:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body: There is no race, everything should be looked. Most likely the container cleanup failed and thus the ip was leaked in the DB.

I don't think so. Looking at the code in https://github.com/containers/common/blob/e5d97e0eea0a35d2b1bf15239a4130e89fee5619/libnetwork/netavark/run.go#L112 we should always delete static ips from the db. Therefore something before that must have failed and never called into that network cleanup code path.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have heard no further action on this issue, I am going to close.  Reopen if you believe there is still more work to be done.

I hit this issue using `4.4.1`:

```
- podman nginx stop
- podman rm nginx
- podman run -d --name nginx -p 8443:8443 -v $HOME/mnt/nginx:/etc/nginx:Z --network=podman --ip=10.88.0.100 nginx:1.23-alpine-slim

Error: IPAM error: requested ip address 10.88.0.100 is already allocated to container ID 1e967bae12d2cf693e9e0ed3fa3593c694db671f994444a2d8bf6d0b942cee13
```

Is there any way I can proceed to remove this IP from Podman's database manually?

I am also getting this issue with podman `4.4.1` (containers are started using `docker-compose`)

```
Error response from daemon: IPAM error: requested ip address 172.22.1.253 is already allocated to container ID 375fc9276b68b0201da0f9ee9eb7a14c50cb2ea58f9b61b1852310e9ba5b37ef
```

I'm on 4.4.2 (netavark 15.0, aardvark-dns 1.5.0) and experience the same
also using docker-compose

```
Creating svi-v42_frontend_1          ... error

ERROR: for svi-v42_frontend_1  Cannot start service frontend: IPAM error: requested ip address 10.89.0.110 is already allocated to container ID aba087df711bb3fedd7499dc3cf7000cace1618c3c0d8374a001ae4c05c78df5

ERROR: for frontend  Cannot start service frontend: IPAM error: requested ip address 10.89.0.110 is already allocated to container ID aba087df711bb3fedd7499dc3cf7000cace1618c3c0d8374a001ae4c05c78df5
ERROR: Encountered errors while bringing up the project.
sagemcom@svi-testlab-hun:~/v4.2.0-RC5/docker$ podman inspect aba087df711bb3fedd7499dc3cf7000cace1618c3c0d8374a001ae4c05c78df5
[]
Error: inspecting object: no such object: "aba087df711bb3fedd7499dc3cf7000cace1618c3c0d8374a001ae4c05c78df5"
```

I use external network, the issue still remains after removing and recreating the network

reproducible...

- start services with `docker-compose up`, make at least one of them unable to start, with something  like "port already allocated"
- fix the port allocation problem
- try to start services again

I think I just encountered the same problem with a rootful container on EL9 using podman 4.4.1.

* Built new image.
* Stopped quadlet container.
* Edited quadlet unit file because the image was renamed.
* Ran `systemctl daemon-reload`.
* `systemctl start` fails with the error `Error: IPAM error: requested ip address 10.88.0.2 is already allocated to container ID buildah-buildah1336789766`.

I made sure I deleted every container shown by `buildah containers --all` and I still got the same error.

A reboot fixed it though by starting from scratch with a blank `/run/containers/networks/ipam.db`.
In my case this happened after the container with that IP encountered a bind error. It's as if the IP is mapped before the bind but not cleaned up if the bind fails?
@Luap99 Can you take a fresh look at this?

I landed some cleanup fixes in the past months that should be in podman v4.6 so please retest.

Without a proper reproducer there is nothing I can do.
Ok lets assume that this is fixed until we have a reproducer.

> I hit this issue using `4.4.1`:
> 
> ```
> - podman nginx stop
> - podman rm nginx
> - podman run -d --name nginx -p 8443:8443 -v $HOME/mnt/nginx:/etc/nginx:Z --network=podman --ip=10.88.0.100 nginx:1.23-alpine-slim
> 
> Error: IPAM error: requested ip address 10.88.0.100 is already allocated to container ID 1e967bae12d2cf693e9e0ed3fa3593c694db671f994444a2d8bf6d0b942cee13
> ```
> 
> Is there any way I can proceed to remove this IP from Podman's database manually?

I managed to remove said IP manually by installing boltbrowser on the machine
`go install github.com/br0xen/boltbrowser@master`

And then manually removing all the records from ipam.db that referenced the deleted container(both IP and container ID)
`./go/bin/boltbrowser /run/user/1000/containers/networks/ipam.db`

I also reloaded the podman network after doing this but I'm not sure if it's strictly necessary. This procedure is probably not a supported use-case anyway
`podman network reload --all`

ps: FWIW my podman version is 4.4.1 and I just happened upon this issue while trying to figure out how to get a container back up without restarting the server
